examples.name: matchmaker
examples.title: Matchmaker
examples.blurb: Three loci wired through the typed bus: a Lobby publishes players onto a queue topic, the Matchmaker counts joins and publishes a match, a MatchLog prints it — each line timestamped by Hale calling JavaScript's Date.now() through @ffi("js"). The whole chain runs as WebAssembly in your browser.

examples.name: sim
examples.title: Live lobby (paced)
examples.blurb: Same idea, animated: an @export locus whose frame() the page drives ~60x/sec. Players trickle in over real time and matches start live — press Run, watch it tick, press Stop. State lives in the locus and persists across frames.

examples.name: jobqueue
examples.title: Job queue (bus)
examples.blurb: A job queue as a service: typed Jobs/Results topics on the bus, a Worker that reacts to each job, a Reporter, and a Submitter. Nobody calls anybody — they publish and subscribe. (Step 3 of the Build a job queue tutorial.)

examples.name: decimal
examples.title: Exact money
examples.blurb: Decimal is a primitive in Hale — exact fixed-point arithmetic, no floating-point dust. Real d-suffixed Decimal math (subtraction, division, compound interest), in your browser.

examples.name: enums
examples.title: Enums & match
examples.blurb: Sum types with exhaustive pattern matching: drop a case from the match and the compiler refuses to build. Here a traffic light cycles through its states.

examples.name: fallible
examples.title: Errors in the type
examples.blurb: No exceptions — a call that can fail returns fallible(E), and you must address it right at the call site with `or` (a default, `or raise` to propagate, or hand it off). Parsing numbers, with defaults and a fallback chain.

examples.name: collections
examples.title: Collections (@form)
examples.blurb: No Vec<T> ceremony — a collection is a locus with a @form annotation, and @form(vec) synthesizes push/get/pop/len/is_empty on it. `get` is fallible (out-of-range), addressed with `or`. Running as WebAssembly in your browser.
